- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/m-outer-3437aaddcdf6922d623e172c2d6f9278.html#url=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F9954378&title=5.%20Finding%20Your%20Message%20%7C%20Modern%20Day%20Geisha%20(Part%202)%20%7C%20The%20Mystery&referrer=&muid=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&sid=fdbd9602-a5fb-4729-889c-fc318f4a977cddf210&version=6&preview=false&__shared_params__[version]=v3

---

##    5\. Finding Your Message | Modern Day Geisha (Part 2) 

# **UPDATE**

Can I tell you what happened to Beki since we did "The Drill Down Method" together a year ago to find her message?

Well.

First, she started doing weekly face to cam videos revealing personally lived, hard truths about the modeling industry.

As she puts it in her Instagram bio (at the time I'm writing this): ["Body positivity and mental health without the sugar coating 🖤"](https://www.instagram.com/rexadams_/) ("real, real")

And don't think this is easy for her because she's "pretty". She has to battle daily with one of the biggest creativity killers there is:

Perfectionism

But she fought. And she's won often lately.

And this religious weekly effort very quickly led to her getting a national campaign with [MISSGUIDED](https://www.missguidedus.com/). They picked her out of hundreds of girls specifically because of how outspoken she was online.

And then more opportunities starting opening up. Like acting.

This is just the beginning for her.

The acceleration of her career happened because she put 3 days aside to ask herself the deep questions.

Instead of hiding behind weekend brunches, mainstream nightlife, or binge-watching Netflix . 

This kind of soul searching is what you need sometimes to wake up your sleeping giant potential.

Find your message

[![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/GxAAP42ESYaSFCLnEH5Q_8a615eafe1eb3a84e8df2b069b107de2.png)](https://www.instagram.com/p/B9q5dJ9nC7m/)

There are people in the hospital wishing they had the opportunity you have right now.

Don't waste it.

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

![Matt Rodriguez](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/8e2a75b8899e544087a9c14bf19a9bec?d=mm&s=70)

Matt Rodriguez

Awaiting Review 

5 months ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/9954378/comments/27752980)

The tumbleweeds feeling has been so relatable. I'm still in the process of finding my message but I can feel it bubbling inside of me. I felt compelled since joining the mystery school to take a video on my morning walks talking about a thought going through my head. It's only been 3 days but it's felt like a weight off my shoulders to stop caring about what people are going to think or who's going to view it and instead just to get it the fuck out into the world. I always had so much judgement for people doing talking head direct to camera style videos but was secretly so envious of them building an audience being in front of the camera while I was too afraid to get in front of one. Cheers to finding your voice

I’ve been in the uncomfortable moment of trying to distill down my message and perfect it so that it resonates with me and me people and holds true for what I do as an artist. It’s going to take a bit longer to get it just right.

oh I LOVE this! Have just looked at what I've been writing down trying to clarify my intention/mandate/message and removed the the negativity. If she's got the courage to get out there then so have I. oh..and her face, the delight when the penny dropped. Wonderful.

![Jessica Isgro](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/282bad7f2bdbf37160ee5606cffc22a3?d=mm&s=70)

Jessica Isgro

Awaiting Review 

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/9954378/comments/22817881)

I can see that when she gave herself that reflective time to discover who she was and discovered what her voice wanted to say, she then gave herself permission to say it. And thats it. Its like it wasn't even in her realm of possibility before to speak her true feelings. I can relate to this because I think how could I deliver these feelings in a way that is worthwhile for everyone else to hear. I liked that she kind of interviewed herself which gave her the access to deliver it from her experience of being the modelling industry. Giving everyone the real deal. Telling people what is behind the scenes. That is interesting! interesting documentary art in my opinion.

Whats my Rant? I love that you said the answer to "what is my message?" "It's not what you wanna do, but how are you going to do it." I'm gonna really give this face cam exercise three days a week, a try. I know they're a positive results that can come from this, yet I put it off every chance I get. Simply because I want to get it right and not look like a desperate video for help (which we all need)

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

![Jonathan Sturgeon](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/c81c4380f74b75aeb92da30067c9b2de?d=mm&s=70)

Jonathan Sturgeon

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/9954378/comments/15371724)

The problem with perfectionism hit too close to home. I know there is a process, and I know that there will always be something that needs tweaking, but I always find myself wanting to get it right on the first try. I appreciate and love the process, but I want to skip to the end and have the results. Obviously that cant happen. Seeing her overcome that problem so completely is inspiring. And her energy is absolutely infectious.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

![Father Bronques](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/6ab90ae46a200706e774b0b9fbeb0e8d?d=mm&s=70) Instructor 

Father Bronques

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/9954378/comments/15412325)

If you want to "skip to the end", you don't love the process. 😂 Jonathan, maybe, you "kind of like" the process...? Maybe you're "intrigued by" the process...? But "love"? Nah, when you love the process, you forget there's even an end... And when you finally do remember, you immediately slow things down so you can smell every one of the roses.
